title: Glädjerus på världens börser efter fredsavtal med Iran
summary_md:
# Glädjerus på världens börser efter fredsavtal med Iran

**Feed**: efn-borsfrukost  
**Published**: 2026-06-15  
**Market Sentiment**: Positive

⏱️ **Time Horizon Key**: ST = <3 months | MT = 3-12 months | LT = >12 months

---

## Episode Thesis

A buoyant Swedish morning markets show: hopes of an Iran peace deal sent Asian, European and US futures higher, with the Stockholm OMX30 up over 2% and oil falling back toward pre-conflict levels near $83. Beyond the macro mood, the episode previews a heavy week of capital markets days (Securitas, Elekta, Atrium Ljungberg) and central bank decisions (Fed, Riksbank — both expected unchanged), plus the successful SpaceX IPO, a Nettel/Infrea merger, the Salix spin-off from Volati, and Intrum's rights trading.

---

## Universe Stocks

### 🟢 SECU-B — Securitas | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 70%

Host long-term shareholder; company delivered on 8% margin target, targets 10%, trades at a relatively low P/E (~13 this year, 12 next).

#### Reasoning

Host expresses clear conviction that Securitas is undervalued relative to demonstrated execution — it has done what it promised, grinding margins up to 8% with a credible path to 10%, yet the market isn't pricing in success. Upcoming London capital markets day on AI/Intelligence-Led Security could be a catalyst. Host believes 'there is more there'.

#### Sell Triggers

- if the 10% margin target is abandoned or pushed out
- if AI/Intelligence-Led Security strategy fails to translate into growth
- if margin progress stalls

> *"jag tycker de har en förhållandevis låg värdering i förhållande till vad de faktiskt har presterat ... jag tror det finns mer där"*

---

### ⛔ EKTA-B — Elekta | **AVOID** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Negative | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 55%

Big miss on last report, stock fell 13% on weak order intake; changed order-recognition criteria and credibility concerns around the order book.

#### Reasoning

Hosts are skeptical: the latest report disappointed badly and the stock dropped 13%, driven largely by weak order intake partly attributable to changed reporting criteria. The market only partly buys the explanation and doesn't fully trust the quality of the order book. The new 'Evo' product has now been out a full quarter, so excuses are running thin — execution needs to pick up before confidence returns.

#### Sell Triggers

- if next-quarter order intake improves materially
- if Evo product gains clear traction
- if management restores credibility on order reporting

> *"aktien gick ner 13% när lagen stängde tror jag så att det är väl ett tecken på att man inte riktigt vågar lita på det"*

---

### 🟢 CEVI — Sella Vision (CellaVision) | **BUY** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: ST  
**Confidence**: 45%

Up 10% after a buy recommendation in Dagens Industri citing a potential share-price doubling and AI angle.

#### Reasoning

This is a cited analyst/media view rather than host conviction: DI issued a buy recommendation pointing to a possible price doubling and an AI angle, and the hosts note that doubling-plus-AI tends to lift a stock. They report it positively but offer no independent thesis, so confidence is modest and the move may be sentiment-driven.

#### Sell Triggers

- if the DI thesis on doubling proves unfounded
- if AI narrative does not translate to fundamentals
- if the initial pop fades

> *"Sella Vision går upp 10 %. De fick en köprekommendation i DI där man pratar om kursdubbling och AI."*

---

### 🟠 ATRLJ-B — Atrium Ljungberg | **WATCH** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Mixed | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 40%

Won a large Ericsson HQ contract (positive) but stock is down ~12% since the announcement amid owner Varma selling and a large NAV discount.

#### Reasoning

Mixed picture ahead of today's capital markets day: the Ericsson headquarters contract is fundamentally positive, but the share hasn't lifted — down ~12% since the announcement, pressured by Finnish owner Varma selling below market and a sizable NAV discount common to the sector. Hosts flag it as worth watching rather than a clear directional call (not the host's core area).

#### Sell Triggers

- if NAV discount widens further
- if more large owners sell down
- if rates back up and pressure property sector

> *"kursen är faktiskt ner ... 12 % sen det här Ericsson-kontraktet presenterades och de har, som många andra i sektorn, en ganska stor substansrabatt"*

---

### 🟠 SKIS-B — Skistar | **WATCH** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Mixed | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 40%

Q3 report due; aggressive ~13% price hikes test customer tolerance, but loyal base and near-monopoly position; staycation trend could help if travel stays expensive.

#### Reasoning

Hosts see a balanced setup: a small seasonal quarter so focus is on forward bookings and next-year pricing. Repeated steep price increases (~13%) risk hitting a pain threshold, but Skistar benefits from a loyal customer base, second-home owners wanting to use their properties, and a near-monopoly in Swedish mountains. A prolonged conflict keeping flights expensive could favor staycation demand. Net: monitor bookings commentary.

#### Sell Triggers

- if forward bookings weaken versus last year
- if price hikes trigger visible demand drop
- if cheaper Alpine alternatives draw customers away

> *"om det skulle fortsätta den här konflikten så kanske det ju också gynnar en spelare som skistar"*

---
